run_metadata.coverage_summary: Live discovery on 2026-06-28 (Sydney) via WebSearch. Tier 1 swept across all nine jurisdictions (Commonwealth/AusTender, NSW eTendering/buy.nsw, Buying for Victoria, QTenders QLD + supply.qld.gov.au migration, Tenders WA, Tenders SA, Tenders Tasmania, NT QTOL, ACT Tenders). Three new open opportunities captured this run: a Commonwealth AusTender RFT (Office of Parliamentary Counsel printing & distribution of Australian Government bills, ATM OPC-2026-02, closing 07-Jul-2026); a new NSW eTendering opportunity (Randwick High School fibre network upgrade via School Infrastructure NSW, closing 17-Jul-2026); and a new Inner West Council EOI (operation & management of the Summer Hill Neighbourhood Centre via VendorPanel, closing 29-Jun-2026). Fifteen still-open / recently-published opportunities from the 2026-06-26 and 2026-06-27 passes were carried forward with refreshed status. Tier 2 (GBE/public sector) re-checked Sydney Water, Water Corporation WA, Main Roads WA, Sydney Trains (newly recorded buyer-originated NSW eTendering channel) and the broader GBE set. Tier 3 (private sector) executed and mandatory: SAP Ariba Discovery / SAP Business Network plus major-buyer portals re-confirmed (BHP, Rio Tinto, Fortescue, Woodside, Santos, Qantas, Coles, Woolworths) and three new corporate/public buyer channels recorded — Telstra (Supplying to Telstra procurement), Brisbane City Council (a council on SAP Ariba, an exception to the usual council VendorPanel pattern) and the Reserve Bank of Australia (Procurement & Suppliers). All corporate opportunities remain behind free supplier registration or invitation-only, so they are recorded as discovered_sources and no corporate tender was fabricated. CRITICAL LIMITATION (unchanged from prior runs): every buyer detail page tried (tenders.gov.au, buy.nsw, sydneywater.com.au) returned HTTP 403 to the fetch tool (bot/anti-scraping protection), so all opportunity detail is from buyer-originated search-result snippets only; no tender document could be opened this run.
gaps: All buyer-originated tender detail pages tried this run (tenders.gov.au ATM pages, buy.nsw, tenders.nsw.gov.au, sydneywater.com.au) returned HTTP 403 to WebFetch (bot/anti-scraping protection). Detail therefore comes from buyer-originated search snippets only and could not be confirmed against the notices' own documents; tender IDs, values, evaluation criteria and contacts remain unverified for most entries.

Conflicting closing date for the Frank Partridge Rest Area potable water tender: the 2026-06-27 run recorded closing 01-Jul-2026, while a fresh buy.nsw snippet this run shows 06-Jul-2026. Kept the prior value (01-Jul) and flagged the discrepancy in notes; the notice could not be opened (403) to resolve it.

WA, QLD, VIC, TAS, NT and ACT Tier 1 portals confirmed live this run but no current opportunity with a concrete closing date could be extracted from snippets (Buying for Victoria, Tenders WA and supply.qld.gov.au list dynamically and were not extractable; QLD is mid-migration to VendorPanel; ACT procurement reforms take effect 01-Jul-2026).

The SA Hydrogen & Renewable Energy Release Areas call for tenders (Gawler Ranges East / Whyalla West) recorded a closing date of 28-Jun-2026, i.e. the run date itself; it may close today. Carried forward as closing-soon and could not be re-confirmed open (Tenders SA detail not openable, 403).

Tier 3 corporate opportunities (SAP Ariba Discovery/SBN; BHP, Rio Tinto, Fortescue, Woodside via Ariba; Santos and Qantas via Coupa; Coles and Woolworths via Ariba; Telstra via its supplier program) are registration-gated or invitation-only and not publicly scrapable; no authenticated supplier session is available, so portals are recorded but no corporate tender entries were fabricated. Management-consultancy RFPs (Accenture/KPMG/Deloitte) remain out of scope (invitation-only).

EstimateOne / construction subcontractor work-packages and the Ausgrid TenderLink BESS Delivery Panel require a free trade/supplier account to view live package closing dates; platforms remain recorded as sources, individual packages not captured.

Brisbane City Council publishes all public tenders only inside its SAP Ariba supplier portal (registration required); current opportunities and closing dates were not extractable without an authenticated session this run.
recommended_seed_additions: Telstra — Supplying to Telstra / About procurement (https://www.telstra.com.au/aboutus/our-company/supplying-to-telstra/about-procurement); national telco corporate buyer; supplier program with RFx via its procurement process.

Brisbane City Council — Supplier portal (SAP Ariba) (https://www.brisbane.qld.gov.au/business/council-tenders-and-market-led-proposals/current-tenders); largest local government in Australia; publishes public tenders via SAP Ariba rather than VendorPanel — a useful exception to the council=VendorPanel assumption.
Sydney Trains — NSW eTendering (https://www.tenders.nsw.gov.au/sydneytrains/); NSW transport GBE; buyer-originated RFT channel on NSW eTendering distinct from the buy.nsw aggregate view.

Reserve Bank of Australia — Procurement & Suppliers (https://www.rba.gov.au/procurement/); Commonwealth public-sector buyer; runs its own procurement and supplier registration outside AusTender for many categories.

tenders.title: Printing and distribution services for Australian Government bills
tenders.description: The Office of Parliamentary Counsel (OPC) has an ongoing requirement for printing and distribution services for Australian Government bills, and is seeking proposals from potential suppliers detailing their capacity to deliver the services.
tenders.deliverables_summary: Provision of printing and distribution services for Australian Government bills, delivered from 17 September 2026 to 16 September 2029, with options to extend for up to a further 2 x 1-year periods. The supplier must demonstrate capacity to print and distribute bills on the timeframes required by the parliamentary process.
key_deliverables: Capacity to deliver services from 17 Sep 2026 to 16 Sep 2029 (plus up to 2 x 1-year extension options)
tenders.eligibility: Open to all suppliers (publicly advertised RFT via AusTender).
tenders.contract_term: 3 years (17 Sep 2026 – 16 Sep 2029) with options to extend up to a further 2 x 1 year periods
tenders.detail_source: AusTender Current ATM View search-result snippet for OPC-2026-02 (title, category 82121500, published 11-Jun-2026, closing 07-Jul-2026 16:00 ACT, contract 17-Sep-2026 to 16-Sep-2029 + 2x1yr options); the ATM detail page returned HTTP 403.
tenders.reasoning: Found via AusTender keyword search for new July-2026 ATMs; buyer-originated on the Commonwealth AusTender portal (Office of Parliamentary Counsel).
tenders.notes: Headline + snippet-derived deliverables and timeline. The ATM detail page and any attached documents could not be opened (HTTP 403), so value, evaluation criteria and contact remain unverified.

tenders.buyer: NSW Department of Education / School Infrastructure NSW
tenders.description: School Infrastructure NSW is seeking a contractor to deliver a fibre network upgrade at Randwick High School.
tenders.eligibility: Open (publicly advertised via NSW eTendering / buy.nsw).
tenders.detail_source: buy.nsw open-opportunities search-result snippet (title, School Infrastructure agency, closing 17-Jul-2026 15:00); detail page returned HTTP 403.
tenders.reasoning: Found via buy.nsw / NSW eTendering open-tenders search for July 2026 closing dates; buyer-originated on the NSW Government procurement portal (School Infrastructure NSW).
tenders.notes: Headline/snippet level only. Closing 17-Jul-2026 15:00 from the buy.nsw listing; detail page not openable (HTTP 403), so RFT number, value, scope and contacts are unverified.

tenders.title: Operation and Management of the Summer Hill Neighbourhood Centre
tenders.description: Inner West Council invites expressions of interest from suitably qualified and experienced organisations for the operation and management of the Summer Hill Neighbourhood Centre.
tenders.deliverables_summary: Operation and ongoing management of the Summer Hill Neighbourhood Centre as a community facility — the successful organisation will run the centre and deliver community programs/services on the Council's behalf.
key_deliverables: Operation of the Summer Hill Neighbourhood Centre
Management of the community facility and its programs/services
requirements: Suitably qualified and experienced organisation (community/neighbourhood centre operation)
tenders.eligibility: Open EOI to suitably qualified and experienced organisations (publicly advertised via VendorPanel).
tenders.submission_method: Lodge via VendorPanel (www.vendorpanel.com.au/innerwest/tenders); enquiries via the VendorPanel online forum
tenders.delivery_location: Summer Hill, Inner West, Sydney, NSW
tenders.detail_source: Inner West Council current-tenders / VendorPanel search-result snippet (title, EOI, closing Mon 29-Jun-2026 10:00 AEST, contact Frank Tambosis); council page returned HTTP 403 to direct fetch.
tenders.reasoning: Found via VendorPanel public-tenders / Inner West Council current-tenders search; buyer-originated (council) on its chosen VendorPanel platform.
tenders.notes: Closing-soon (29-Jun-2026 10:00 AEST). Contact name from the listing snippet; email/phone routed via the VendorPanel forum. Tender reference, value and full scope unverified (page not openable, 403).

Software for footage storage and retrieval
Subscription services for safety-related footage management

tenders.title: Air Transport and Remote Health Services for Regional and Rural NSW (Far West LHD)
tenders.buyer: NSW Ministry of Health / Far West Local Health District
tenders.description: NSW Health is seeking a provider (or providers) to deliver air transport and a bundle of remote health services across regional and rural NSW, anchored in the Far West Local Health District.
tenders.deliverables_summary: Provision of inter-hospital patient transfers and fixed-wing transport for clinicians, plus delivery of remote/rural health services including medical primary health care clinics, remote oral health services, and drug and alcohol information and counselling services across the Far West Local Health District and regional/rural NSW.
key_deliverables: Inter-hospital patient transfers across regional and rural NSW
Fixed-wing transport for clinicians to provide health services in rural and remote communities
Medical primary health care clinics in Far West LHD
Remote oral health services in Far West LHD
Drug and alcohol information and counselling services in Far West LHD
tenders.eligibility: Open (publicly advertised RFT via NSW eTendering).
tenders.delivery_location: Far West Local Health District, regional and rural NSW
tenders.detail_source: Scope from a GovMarket aggregator listing snippet (discovery signal only); buyer-originated NSW eTendering page could not be opened (HTTP 403).
tenders.reasoning: Carried forward; originally discovered via an aggregator listing used only as a discovery signal and recorded against the buyer-originated source (NSW eTendering / NSW Ministry of Health). Aggregator is NOT treated as the source of truth.
tenders.notes: Carry-forward, still open (closing 16-Jul-2026). Service scope is snippet-derived via an aggregator and still needs confirmation on the buyer's own NSW eTendering notice; RFT number, value and contacts unverified.

key_deliverables: Graphic design and digital marketing materials

tenders.eligibility: Open to all suppliers (publicly advertised RFT).
